home.title: Kalaam Lah Lazimah — Essays on Awareness & Values
home.metaDesc: Thoughtful essays on awareness, values, and ethics by Ziad Amr.
home.heroTag: Not every word needs to be spoken… but some words must be. Quiet essays on awareness, values, and ethics, written close to the heart.
home.eyebrowMagazine: An Arabic magazine for awareness & values
home.heroLead: Here we write the words that deserve your pause — close to the heart, clear in meaning, and respectful of your mind and time.
home.manifestoText: We write quietly, choosing meaning over noise, believing that a kind word can change a person entirely.
home.value1Text: A single article that leaves an impact is better than a hundred passing pieces of content.
home.value2Text: Simple, close language — without losing the value of the idea or the reader's respect.
home.value3Text: The design here is calm — guiding your eye to the word rather than competing with it.

about.title: About Kalaam Lah Lazimah
about.lead: Not every word must be spoken… but some words must be.
about.p1: Kalaam Lah Lazimah is a purposeful Arabic essay project written by Ziad Amr. The idea is simple: to restore weight to the word, and to write on awareness, values, and ethics in a language close to the heart — without pretense, without complexity, but with care for meaning and depth of thought.
about.p2: We write on topics that touch our daily lives: digital awareness, ethics and spiritual refinement, values and society, human relationships, and everything that touches the heart and conscience. The goal is not preaching so much as raising questions and offering a vision — religious, social, and human — that helps the reader pause with themselves for a moment.
about.p3: I'm Ziad Amr, a writer interested in calm words and deep meaning. You can find me on my personal site, where the rest of my work and writing lives:
about.p4: If you have feedback, a topic suggestion, or want to share an idea — you can reach me through my personal site. Your opinion matters, and any simple conversation opens a door to deeper understanding.
about.thanks: Thank you for reading this far. Let's continue.
about.metaDesc: An introduction to the Kalaam Lah Lazimah project and its author Ziad Amr.

start.title: Start here
start.tag: If you're new to Kalaam Lah Lazimah, this page walks you through how to get the most out of the site and quickly find what matters to you.
start.p1_1: Purposeful Arabic essays across three main axes: Digital Awareness (our mindful relationship with social media and trends), Values & Society (Islam's view of human relationships and dignity), and Ethics & Spirituality (building the self and training it in virtue). Each essay is written close to the heart, with Quran verses, hadiths, and real-world examples.
start.p1_2: Each article has a tashkeel button when a voweled version is available — for when you want to read the original text with diacritics. At the end of each article there are interactive exercises: multiple-choice questions with instant feedback, and i'rab drills with a “show model answer” button. There's also a teacher mode for printing without answers, font-size buttons to tune your reading comfort, and a save-for-later feature that stores articles in localStorage.
start.h1_3: How do you choose what fits you?
start.p1_3: Each article carries a level badge (Beginner / Intermediate / Advanced). If you're new, start with “Beginner” articles and work your way up. You can also browse any category from the top or from an article card, and see all its articles on one page. Articles belonging to a series are linked with “previous / next lesson” and a path you can follow.
start.p1_4: On any page, press <kbd>/</kbd> or <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>K</kbd> to open the search box. Press <kbd>Esc</kbd> to close it. On an article page, the toolbar above the content gives you full control over the reading experience.
start.metaDesc: A starter's guide to Kalaam Lah Lazimah: what you'll find, reading features, and how to pick what fits you.
